title: 4-colour screen-print separation pack — white underbase + lavender/cobalt/deep-purple spot plates via masked halftone, dot-gain choke, licensed grunge + VHS alt colourway, vectorized lettering plate, and a real Illustrator-rendered separation/registration sheet driven by a spot-colour CSV

fix.prompt_delta: Replace '—' in pantone column with literal string 'N/A' (ASCII only); re-emit spot_colour.csv with that substitution.

deductions: image 3 (source_layers[1] clouds-only) has a visible black border/vignette frame around the cloud mass — image_select_subject will include border pixels, polluting the cloud mask used for halftone step
image 2 (source_layers[0]) lettering layer: upper half is pure black with no lettering pixels visible — image_vectorize on this asset will produce a near-empty vector plate

fix.problem: Hard black border vignette around clouds will corrupt image_select_subject mask for halftone step
fix.prompt_delta: Render clouds-only layer as a transparent-background PNG (no black canvas, no border frame); cloud forms only on alpha channel so image_select_subject isolates clean cloud silhouette.

deductions: Choke decision records choke_px=2 and underbase_choke_px=1.5 but does not specify whether gaussian sigma equals choke_px directly or is derived — agent may misinterpret
No assumption recorded for the 1024px source dims vs. brief's ~4500px spec; agent may not know to upscale before halftone to avoid coarse dot artifacts
All assumptions are justified and align with standard workflows
No client input was wrongly demoted to an assumption
All assumptions justified and industry-standard
No real input wrongly demoted to assumption

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): The approved artwork and all source layers are only 1024x1024px — far below the ~4500px specified in the brief and nowhere near sufficient resolution for film-output halftone separations at 55 LPI without visible pixelation and aliasing on the dot field. I will not send underresolution files to press.

AGENT CHOKE on 'approved_artwork': image_apply_halftone runs on a 1024px raster; at 55 LPI the dot grid resolves to roughly 18px per dot cell, leaving almost no headroom for the 2px gaussian choke pass — the choke radius consumes the dot shoulder entirely, collapsing shadow dots and destroying the separation before it reaches vectorize or document_merge_data_vector.
